mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals a sharp deterioration in labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.5pp, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01 during contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side constraints and weakening manufacturing activity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns do not turn negative following INDPRO declines, or the signal reverses direction during supply shock regimes without statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in the federal funds rate alongside an unemployment rate below 4.0% indicate accommodative monetary policy without labor market deterioration, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines while UNRATE remains below 4.0%
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of confirmed recession onsets, or the signal fails to predict direction with a Rank IC above 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no positive correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 consistently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation without a corresponding adjustment in the federal funds rate signals real rate compression and policy lag, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances matching the trigger, or the interaction term loses predictive power during inflationary periods.
